goal: All executor/reviewer schemas accept model_unavailable; auditor schemas accept model_unavailable but exclude transient
description: Validates that failure_classification enum is consistent across all schema families. Standard executor and reviewer schemas accept [transient, fixable, needs_replan, escalate, model_unavailable]. Auditor schemas accept [fixable, needs_replan, escalate, model_unavailable] but MUST reject transient (read-only reviewers have no flake-retry semantic).

positive_cases.label: standard-schema-model-unavailable
positive_cases.description: Any executor schema with failure_classification=model_unavailable must be schema-valid

positive_cases.label: auditor-schema-model-unavailable
positive_cases.description: Auditor schemas with failure_classification=model_unavailable must be schema-valid (admitted despite transient exclusion because model_unavailable substitutes the model, not the prompt)

positive_cases.label: standard-schema-transient
positive_cases.description: Standard executor schemas must continue to accept transient

negative_cases.label: auditor-schema-transient-rejected
negative_cases.description: NEGATIVE CONTROL: auditor schemas must reject transient — read-only reviewers have no flake-retry semantic
negative_cases.rationale: transient is structurally excluded from plan-auditor and assumption-verifier schemas. model_unavailable is admitted as a separate class that substitutes the model (not the prompt) and does not carry the flake-retry implication.
